refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable the operator (on-site or remote) to execute earthmoving tasks with measurable productivity of ≥ productivity_target (m³/h or ton/h) during representative operations over the agreed evaluation period, while maintaining compliance with worksite safety plan and operational policies.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-001 is a high-level acceptance criterion that conflates productivity measurement with operational capability. Refinement separates the measurement/verification aspect from the capability delivery aspect, and explicitly acknowledges dual operator modes (on-site and tele-operated) as distinct execution contexts. This clarification is essential because productivity depends on both machine capability and operator effectiveness, which differ between direct and remote control.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Conduct representative operations trials under agreed conditions (soil type, task profile, weather, operator experience level). Measure actual output (m³/h or ton/h) and compare against productivity_target. Log operator mode (on-site vs. remote), task type, and environmental conditions to establish traceability.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The Vehicle shall provide earthmoving capability (bucket capacity, cycle time, reach, force) sufficient to achieve productivity_target under nominal operating conditions.
FR-NGHE-001.2: The Teleoperation subsystem shall enable remote operator control with latency, fidelity, and situational awareness sufficient to achieve ≥ 85% of on-site operator productivity.
FR-NGHE-001.3: The Safety Services subsystem shall enforce worksite safety plan constraints (geofencing, speed limits, collision avoidance) without reducing productivity below productivity_target.
FR-NGHE-001.4: The NGHE Solution shall provide real-time productivity telemetry (cycle count, material moved, task progress) to enable operator and site manager visibility.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Integrate high-capacity hydraulic excavator with optimized bucket geometry, cycle time ≤ target_cycle_time, and operator interface (cab or remote) tuned for task execution speed.

DP-B: Deploy dual-mode control architecture (on-site joystick + remote teleoperation) with low-latency network link and high-fidelity video/sensor feedback to maintain operator situational awareness across both modes.
DP-C: Implement real-time task monitoring and safety constraint enforcement via Safety Services, with predictive alerts to prevent safety violations while maintaining task flow.

DP-D: Establish telemetry pipeline (Vehicle → Network Link → Teleoperation/Safety Services) to stream productivity metrics (cycle count, material volume, task progress) to operator and site manager dashboards.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-Integrated: Combine DP-A (machine capability), DP-B (dual-mode control with low-latency network), DP-C (safety constraint enforcement), and DP-D (telemetry pipeline) into a cohesive system architecture. The Vehicle provides raw earthmoving capability; Teleoperation enables remote execution with fidelity preservation; Safety Services enforces constraints; and Tasking/Telemetry interfaces enable real-time visibility and control.

potential_sources: Latency coupling: Remote operator productivity depends on network link latency and video/sensor feedback fidelity. Mitigation: specify max latency (e.g., <200ms round-trip) and video frame rate (e.g., ≥30 fps).

Safety-productivity coupling: Safety constraint enforcement (geofencing, speed limits) may reduce productivity if overly conservative. Mitigation: tune safety thresholds via site-specific safety plan and validate in trials.

Operator mode coupling: On-site vs. remote operator productivity differs; remote mode may not achieve 100% parity. Mitigation: set realistic remote productivity target (e.g., 85% of on-site) and validate empirically.
coupling_check.notes: The integrated DP is decoupled in the sense that each sub-DP addresses a distinct functional aspect (machine, control, safety, telemetry). However, cross-domain couplings exist at the system level (latency affects control fidelity, safety affects task speed). These are managed via interface specifications and validation trials, not architectural redesign.

assumptions_risks: ASSUMPTION: Network link provides sufficient bandwidth and latency for real-time teleoperation (video, sensor data, control commands). RISK: If network is congested or unreliable, remote operator productivity will degrade. MITIGATION: Specify network SLA (latency, jitter, packet loss) and conduct trials in representative network conditions.

ASSUMPTION: Operator (on-site or remote) is trained and experienced. RISK: Untrained operators may not achieve productivity_target regardless of machine capability. MITIGATION: Include operator training and certification in acceptance criteria.

ASSUMPTION: Worksite safety plan is well-defined and compatible with productivity_target. RISK: If safety constraints are overly restrictive, productivity_target may be unachievable. MITIGATION: Engage Safety Manager early to validate safety plan feasibility.

ASSUMPTION: Productivity_target is achievable with current hydraulic excavator technology and operator skill. RISK: Target may be unrealistic for soil/task conditions. MITIGATION: Conduct feasibility study with representative equipment and operators.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Decompose DP-Integrated into explicit sub-DPs for each FR sub-requirement (FR-NGHE-001.1 through 001.4) to enable independent design and verification. Specify network latency and video fidelity requirements as design constraints for Teleoperation DP. Define safety constraint tuning process (site-specific safety plan → geofencing/speed limits → validation trials) as part of DP-C.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Top-level system integration boundary encompassing Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, and their interconnections via Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, and Network Link interfaces. Responsible for end-to-end productivity delivery and acceptance criterion verification.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Site Manager or Tele-operator provides task definition (location, material type, target volume) to NGHE Solution. Tasking interface carries task parameters and priority.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution streams real-time productivity metrics (cycle count, material moved, task progress, machine state) to Site Manager dashboard and Tele-operator console. Telemetry interface carries time-series data.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Safety Services subsystem issues alerts (geofence violation, collision risk, speed limit exceeded) to Operator and Site Manager. SafetyAlert interface carries safety event notifications.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Bidirectional network connection enabling remote teleoperation (control commands from Tele-operator to Vehicle, video/sensor feedback from Vehicle to Tele-operator). Network Link carries real-time control and sensor data.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the top-level system boundary. All sub-components (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) and their interfaces are contained within this module. No diffusion risk.

Vehicle module provides earthmoving capability (DP-A) but does not own Teleoperation, Safety Services, or telemetry pipeline. Productivity depends on Vehicle capability AND operator control fidelity AND safety constraint enforcement. Assigning FR-NGHE-001 to Vehicle alone would diffuse responsibility for productivity across multiple modules.

Teleoperation module enables remote control (DP-B) but does not own Vehicle capability, Safety Services, or telemetry. Remote operator productivity depends on machine capability, network latency, and safety constraints. Assigning FR-NGHE-001 to Teleoperation alone would diffuse responsibility.

Safety Services enforces constraints (DP-C) but does not own Vehicle capability, Teleoperation, or telemetry. Productivity depends on machine and operator, not safety alone. Assigning FR-NGHE-001 to Safety Services alone would diffuse responsibility significantly.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The productivity KPI (FR-NGHE-001) is a system-level acceptance criterion that requires integration of Vehicle capability, Teleoperation fidelity, Safety Services constraint enforcement, and real-time telemetry. No single sub-module (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) can independently deliver productivity; all must work together. Therefore, NGHE Solution is the correct IM boundary for this FR. Sub-modules own specific DPs (machine capability, remote control, safety enforcement) but NGHE Solution owns the integrated system performance.
containment_risks: RISK: NGHE Solution is a high-level integration boundary, not a concrete implementation module. Detailed ownership of sub-components (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) must be clarified at the next decomposition level. MITIGATION: Decompose FR-NGHE-001 into sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-001.1 through 001.4) and assign each to appropriate sub-module.

RISK: Productivity measurement and verification require coordination across multiple sub-modules (Vehicle telemetry, Teleoperation operator feedback, Safety Services constraint logs). Diffusion of measurement responsibility could lead to incomplete or inconsistent data. MITIGATION: Establish clear telemetry pipeline ownership (likely Teleoperation or Safety Services) and define data schema for productivity metrics.

RISK: Productivity target may be unachievable if any sub-module (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) is inadequate. Acceptance criterion verification requires end-to-end testing, not unit testing of sub-modules. MITIGATION: Plan system-level acceptance trials with representative operations and all sub-modules integrated.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Assign FR-NGHE-001 to NGHE Solution as the system-level acceptance criterion. Decompose into sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-001.1 through 001.4) and assign each to appropriate sub-module (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services). Establish clear ownership of telemetry pipeline and productivity measurement (recommend Teleoperation or Safety Services as owner). Define acceptance trial protocol and success criteria at NGHE Solution level.

fr_dp_gate.reason: Refined FR-NGHE-001 clearly states the productivity requirement (≥ productivity_target m³/h or ton/h) and context (representative operations, agreed evaluation period, compliance with safety plan). Selected DP-Integrated provides a coherent solution approach: Vehicle capability (DP-A) + dual-mode control (DP-B) + safety constraint enforcement (DP-C) + telemetry pipeline (DP-D). The DP logically addresses the FR without over-specification or under-specification. Coupling risks (latency, safety-productivity trade-off, operator mode differences) are identified and manageable via interface specifications and validation trials.

diffusion_risks: Productivity ownership is distributed across Vehicle (capability), Teleoperation (control fidelity), and Safety Services (constraint enforcement). No single sub-module owns end-to-end productivity. MITIGATION: NGHE Solution owns the integrated system performance; sub-modules own specific DPs. Telemetry pipeline must be clearly owned (recommend Teleoperation or Safety Services).

Acceptance criterion verification requires system-level testing (all sub-modules integrated). Unit testing of Vehicle, Teleoperation, or Safety Services alone is insufficient. MITIGATION: Plan acceptance trials at NGHE Solution level with representative operations.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the correct IM boundary because it encompasses all sub-modules and their interfaces. Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services are sub-modules within NGHE Solution, not alternative IM boundaries for this FR. Containment score for NGHE Solution is 100%; diffusion scores for sub-modules (Vehicle 60%, Teleoperation 65%, Safety Services 75%) confirm that no sub-module alone can deliver the FR.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of FR-NGHE-001 is feasible via representative operations trials: measure actual productivity (m³/h or ton/h) under agreed conditions and compare against productivity_target. Trials must include both on-site and remote operator modes, representative soil/task types, and compliance with worksite safety plan. Telemetry pipeline (Telemetry interface) must provide real-time productivity metrics (cycle count, material moved, task progress) for measurement. Verification is system-level (not unit-level) and requires integration of all sub-modules.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: FR-NGHE-001 is allocated to NGHE Solution as the system-level acceptance criterion. Sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-001.1 through 001.4) are allocated to Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, and NGHE Solution (telemetry) respectively. Each sub-FR has a clear DP and IM boundary. Allocation is clear and traceable.

recommended_revisions: Decompose FR-NGHE-001 into sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-001.1 through 001.4) and assign each to appropriate sub-module (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, NGHE Solution). This enables independent design and verification of sub-components while maintaining system-level acceptance criterion.

Specify network latency and video fidelity requirements as design constraints for Teleoperation DP. Example: max latency <200ms round-trip, video frame rate ≥30 fps, resolution ≥720p. These constraints are critical for remote operator productivity.
Define safety constraint tuning process: site-specific safety plan → geofencing/speed limits → validation trials. This ensures that Safety Services DP does not unnecessarily reduce productivity.

Establish telemetry pipeline ownership and data schema. Recommend Teleoperation or Safety Services as owner. Define productivity metrics (cycle count, material volume, task progress, operator mode, environmental conditions) and data format.

Plan acceptance trial protocol: representative operations, agreed evaluation period, operator experience level, soil/task types, weather conditions, on-site and remote operator modes. Define success criteria (≥ productivity_target, compliance with safety plan, operator safety and comfort).

decision.justification: FR-NGHE-001 is a high-level system acceptance criterion that requires decomposition into sub-FRs addressing specific aspects of productivity delivery: Vehicle capability (FR-NGHE-001.1), Teleoperation fidelity (FR-NGHE-001.2), Safety Services constraint enforcement (FR-NGHE-001.3), and telemetry pipeline (FR-NGHE-001.4). Each sub-FR has a distinct DP and IM boundary. Decomposition is necessary to enable independent design, verification, and ownership of sub-components while maintaining traceability to the system-level acceptance criterion. The refined FR, selected DP, and assigned IM are sound at the current level, but further decomposition is required to drive detailed design and implementation.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The Vehicle shall provide earthmoving capability (bucket capacity, cycle time, reach, force) sufficient to achieve productivity_target under nominal operating conditions.
FR-NGHE-001.2: The Teleoperation subsystem shall enable remote operator control with latency, fidelity, and situational awareness sufficient to achieve ≥ 85% of on-site operator productivity.
FR-NGHE-001.3: The Safety Services subsystem shall enforce worksite safety plan constraints (geofencing, speed limits, collision avoidance) without reducing productivity below productivity_target.
FR-NGHE-001.4: The NGHE Solution shall provide real-time productivity telemetry (cycle count, material moved, task progress) to enable operator and site manager visibility.

assumptions: Network link provides sufficient bandwidth and latency for real-time teleoperation (video, sensor data, control commands). Network SLA: latency <200ms round-trip, jitter <50ms, packet loss <0.1%.
Operator (on-site or remote) is trained and experienced. Operator training and certification are included in acceptance criteria.
Worksite safety plan is well-defined and compatible with productivity_target. Safety Manager validates safety plan feasibility early in design.
Productivity_target is achievable with current hydraulic excavator technology and operator skill. Feasibility study conducted with representative equipment and operators.
Remote operator productivity is expected to be ≥ 85% of on-site operator productivity due to latency and situational awareness limitations. This is a realistic target based on industry experience.
Productivity is measured as m³/h or ton/h during representative operations over an agreed evaluation period (e.g., 8-hour workday, 5-day week, 4-week trial).
Safety Services constraint enforcement (geofencing, speed limits, collision avoidance) is tuned to site-specific safety plan and does not unnecessarily reduce productivity.
open_risks: RISK: Network latency or congestion during acceptance trials could degrade remote operator productivity below target. MITIGATION: Conduct trials in representative network conditions; specify network SLA; have fallback to on-site operation if network fails.

RISK: Operator skill and experience vary; untrained operators may not achieve productivity_target. MITIGATION: Include operator training and certification in acceptance criteria; use experienced operators for trials.

RISK: Worksite safety plan constraints may be overly restrictive, making productivity_target unachievable. MITIGATION: Engage Safety Manager early; validate safety plan feasibility; tune safety thresholds via trials.

RISK: Soil/task conditions during trials may differ from representative conditions, affecting productivity measurement. MITIGATION: Define representative conditions clearly (soil type, moisture, task profile); conduct trials in multiple conditions; log environmental data.

RISK: Acceptance criterion verification requires system-level testing (all sub-modules integrated). Unit testing of sub-modules alone is insufficient. MITIGATION: Plan system-level acceptance trials; ensure all sub-modules are integrated and tested together.

RISK: Productivity measurement may be incomplete or inconsistent if telemetry pipeline ownership is unclear. MITIGATION: Establish clear ownership of telemetry pipeline (recommend Teleoperation or Safety Services); define data schema and validation procedures.
evidence_needed: Feasibility study: Can productivity_target be achieved with current hydraulic excavator technology and operator skill? Conduct trials with representative equipment and operators.
Network SLA validation: Does network link meet latency, jitter, and packet loss requirements? Conduct network testing in representative conditions.
Safety plan validation: Is worksite safety plan compatible with productivity_target? Engage Safety Manager; validate safety constraint tuning via trials.
Operator training: Are operators trained and certified? Establish training program and certification criteria.

Acceptance trial protocol: Define representative operations, evaluation period, operator experience level, soil/task types, weather conditions, on-site and remote operator modes. Define success criteria.
Telemetry pipeline specification: Define productivity metrics (cycle count, material volume, task progress, operator mode, environmental conditions), data format, and validation procedures.
System integration testing: Are all sub-modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) integrated and tested together? Conduct system-level acceptance trials.
